item.title: Granite-rich Mutoko left behind as corporate responsibility remains voluntary
item.description: Surrounded by the highly sought-after granite stone that has been exported to Europe and Asia for more than four decades, Gurure Secondary School in Mutoko North has no electricity, no water, insufficient classrooms, and an administration block that has been under construction for the past 15 years. The school’s administration block has been under construction [...] The post Granite-rich Mutoko left behind as corporate responsibility remains voluntary appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe waives import duty for South Africa returnees, imposes strict conditions to curb abuse
item.description: GOVERNMENT has announced a special duty waiver for Zimbabweans returning from South Africa, but has imposed strict conditions aimed at preventing “abuse by non-qualifying individuals”. Addressing journalists in Harare, Monday, Information Minister Soda Zhemu said the waiver covers personal and household goods, commercial equipment and one motor vehicle per returnee. However, he said only vehicles [...] The post Zimbabwe waives import duty for South Africa returnees, imposes strict conditions to curb abuse appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Kaia’s brilliant century gives Zimbabwe commanding advantage over Bangladesh
item.description: CricketWorld Zimbabwe seized complete control on Day 2 of the first Test against Bangladesh, piling up 410 before reducing the tourists to 140 all out. Bangladesh finished the day on 40/1 in their second innings, still 230 runs behind. The day belonged to Zimbabwe, whose batting depth and patience gave them a commanding platform. Innocent [...] The post Kaia’s brilliant century gives Zimbabwe commanding advantage over Bangladesh appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Decade of data, same old failure: Zimbabwe’s public institutions are still letting citizens down
item.description: As the country celebrates a record customer satisfaction score, councils report a near-28 negative recommendation rating and public hospitals fail patients at every turn. A new national survey asks: who is this progress actually for? Ten years of measurement. Fifteen thousand voices. Twenty-five sectors assessed across every province of Zimbabwe. And yet, at the heart [...] The post Decade of data, same old failure: Zimbabwe’s public institutions are still letting citizens down appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe repatriates 3,624 from South Africa, blames profiling delays

item.title: Zim Repatriates Over 3 600 Citizens as Govt Responds to South Africa Crisis @263Chat
item.description: The government says more than 3 600 citizens displaced by anti-immigrant unrest in South Africa have returned home with authorities stepping up efforts to support those still stranded amid ongoing security checks and border processing delays. In a statement, Minister of Information, Publicity and Broadcasting [...] The post Zim Repatriates Over 3 600 Citizens as Govt Responds to South Africa Crisis appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Beyond the Uniform: How Female Rangers Are Transforming Conservation in Zimbabwe @263Chat
item.description: By Edith Mugabe As dawn breaks over Zimbabwe’s wilderness, Sharai Viola Tunhira, a female ranger working with Akashinga, prepares for another day on patrol. Dressed in her uniform and equipped for the demands of the field, she is part of a growing force of women [...] The post Beyond the Uniform: How Female Rangers Are Transforming Conservation in Zimbabwe appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: 30 June shutdown: ANC boss Fikile Mbalula blames Zimbabwe government and Jacob Zuma for causing South Africa’s problems (VIDEO)
item.description: Johannesburg – Can a single date on a calendar bring an entire nation to a complete standstill? Hello everyone, my name is John Brown. The question of whether South Africa is prepared for the events of 30 June 2026 is being asked across the country. This date marks a deadline set by the March and [...] The post 30 June shutdown: ANC boss Fikile Mbalula blames Zimbabwe government and Jacob Zuma for causing South Africa’s problems (VIDEO) first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Police Break Silence On Detective Who Died Days After Alleged Secret Visit To Police Training Academy @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: ZRP Speaks On Detective Who Died In Accident Days After Allegedly Sneaking Into Police Training Academy The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has responded to widespread social media claims surrounding the death of Detective Sergeant Milika Laison, saying the officer died in a road accident days after he allegedly sneaked into the Ntabazinduna Police Academy. The [...] The post Police Break Silence On Detective Who Died Days After Alleged Secret Visit To Police Training Academy appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
